objectives: Review hand-written Rust code across 7 crates for quality, maintainability, and correctness
Check clean code principles, SOLID, DRY, naming, error handling, documentation, and Rust idioms
Cover new code added since last review: pattern_codegen, typed.rs, emit/validate.rs
constraints: Exclude generated/ directories from review scope
Focus on mx20022-validate, mx20022-translate, mx20022-parse, mx20022-model/common, mx20022-codegen, mx20022-cli
important_facts: Prior review from 2026-03-04T00:10 remains fully valid -- no prior issues were fixed
New since last review: pattern_codegen.rs (XSD->Rust inline pattern compiler), typed.rs (constraint bridge), emit/validate.rs (Validatable codegen)
mod97 duplicated verbatim in iban.rs:111-118 and lei.rs:111-118 -- confirmed still present

extract_element/extract_all_elements duplicated between cli/validate.rs:73-98 and schemes/xml_scan.rs -- confirmed still present; cli version also has a third helper extract_all_attributes that is NOT in xml_scan
parse_year in datetime.rs:128 allocates Vec<u8> via to_vec() unnecessarily -- confirmed still present
ValidateError::Parse used as exit-code signal in cli/validate.rs:265 -- confirmed still present
ValidationResult.errors field is pub Vec<ValidationError> -- confirmed still public

FedNow amount validation logic duplicated between xml path (validate:171-220) and typed path (validate_pacs008_typed:379-427) -- confirmed; decimal_ok check + parse_amount_cents block is identical 40-line block
helpers.rs typed to pacs008 types only with explicit note at line 1-7; pacs009/camt053 mappings must copy-paste
RuleRegistry::validate_all uses HashMap iteration (non-deterministic order in error output)
pattern_codegen.rs: parse_char_class has duplicated range-parsing arms (escaped vs unescaped, lines 122-175); 30 lines of structurally identical code
pattern_codegen.rs: Quantifier::Range(0, usize::MAX) used to represent '*' (star) but usize::MAX is semantically odd; a dedicated ZeroOrMore variant would be cleaner

pattern_codegen.rs: debug_assert_eq! used in parse_char_class/parse_group to verify invariants (good), but if caller passes a non-[ byte the assert fires in debug; callers are internal so this is safe
pattern_codegen.rs: emit_variable_length emits an IIFE closure for the validator body -- this is correct but unusual; worth a comment explaining why (avoids early-return from outer fn)
typed.rs: validate_message free fn on IsoMessage trait already covers the same case as validate_message free fn in typed.rs -- names collide conceptually but are different types, acceptable

FedNowValidator.max_amount stored as f64; max_cents computed via (f64 * 100) as u64 -- has explicit allow(cast_possible_truncation) and a code comment justifying it; still a latent footgun for non-round-dollar custom limits
decisions_made: No code changes made - review only
Severity classification for new issues: High=2, Suggestions=4 (cumulative across both reviews: High=6, Suggestions=12)
pattern_codegen range-arm duplication is HIGH priority (new finding)
validate_all nondeterminism was flagged in prior review; still present
risks_issues: DRY: mod97 duplicated in iban.rs and lei.rs -- low risk but maintenance burden
DRY: XML scan helpers duplicated in cli/validate.rs and schemes/xml_scan.rs
DRY: FedNow amount validation logic duplicated between xml and typed paths
DRY: pattern_codegen.rs parse_char_class range-parsing arm duplicated (escaped vs unescaped)
API: ValidateError::Parse used as exit-code signal -- misleading semantics
API: ValidationResult.errors is pub Vec -- external mutation possible
Bug risk: parse_year allocates Vec<u8> unnecessarily
Bug risk: helpers.rs only covers pacs008 types -- pacs009/camt053 must copy-paste
Behavioral: validate_all has non-deterministic error ordering (HashMap)
Design: Quantifier::Range(0, usize::MAX) encodes star quantifier -- magic value

iteration.summary: Second full pass over the codebase after three new commits (newtype construction validation, XSD pattern compiler, post-audit hardening). The new pattern_codegen.rs module is well-structured but has a 30-line duplicated range-parsing arm inside parse_char_class (escaped vs unescaped paths are structurally identical). typed.rs is clean and small with no issues. All previously flagged DRY violations and the ValidateError::Parse API problem remain unfixed. No new critical issues introduced.
outputs_produced: Full code review report (in assistant response)
Updated context file superseding prior review
next_actions: Extract mod97 into crates/mx20022-validate/src/rules/checkdigit.rs (or similar) and import from both iban.rs and lei.rs
Remove duplicate extract_element/extract_all_elements from cli/validate.rs; add extract_all_attributes to xml_scan.rs and re-export
Extract FedNow amount block into a shared fn validate_amount_range called from both xml and typed paths
Deduplicate parse_char_class range arm by extracting a parse_one_char_or_escape helper
Replace Quantifier::Range(0, usize::MAX) with a ZeroOrMore variant
Fix parse_year to operate directly on the byte slice without Vec allocation
Add a ValidationFailure variant to ValidateError replacing the Parse misuse
Consider making ValidationResult::errors private with a fn findings(&self) -> &[ValidationError] accessor

mentions.relevance_note: Security audit may overlap with pattern_codegen correctness (XSD patterns used for BICFI/IBAN newtype validation in production)
Test coverage audit should verify parse_char_class is tested for escaped vs unescaped range paths independently
